title: Bulk actions over a multi-selection — placement of the batch-apply binding
summary: Prior-art survey grounding decision #1423 (surfaced by the production-app teardown lens #1404, Linear walk): selecting several rows reveals a contextual action bar offering operations applied to the WHOLE selection (move, assign, label, delete), with select-all / clear-selection affordances and an a11y announcement of 'N selected'. Recurs across Gmail, Linear, Google Drive, Notion, file managers. WE owns the pieces but not the composition: selection models the choice, command/action model a single invocation. Native grounding: the batch-apply surface decomposes onto native/ARIA primitives — 'N selected' = an ARIA live region (aria-live=polite/role=status; polite, must not interrupt); the action bar = role=toolbar (APG Toolbar, one tab stop, roving tabindex — WE already owns this); select-all header = a tri-state checkbox aria-checked=mixed (some-but-not-all -> mixed; click on mixed -> select all, click again -> clear); the selectable set = listbox/grid multi-select (aria-multiselectable + per-item aria-selected, what SelectionBehavior projects); post-action focus return = a managed-focus (focus-delegation) concern. Load-bearing finding: Polaris AND Carbon ship bulk-actions as a first-class NAMED API and the pattern recurs identically — Shopify Polaris names bulkActions + promotedBulkActions (1-2 promoted out of the overflow menu) on IndexTable/ResourceList; IBM Carbon names TableBatchActions/TableBatchAction (a batch bar overlaying the toolbar the moment >=1 row is selected, getBatchActionProps exposes selectedRows, live count); recurs across Gmail/Linear/Drive/Notion — the COMPOSITION is the standardizable thing, not the pieces. Finding 2: select-all is a two-scope concept — 'all on this page' vs 'select all N matching' (Gmail's header checkbox selects the page then surfaces 'select all that match this search'; MUI X checkboxSelectionVisibleOnly + keepNonExistentRowsSelected) so the selection set a bulk action fans across may be materialized rows OR a predicate/exclusion-set over an unmaterialized set. Finding 3: bulk actions are explicitly a composition over selection + toolbar, not a built-in (MUI X recipe: read selectedRows in a custom Toolbar; Ant Table rowSelection selectedRowKeys + custom selections). WE-tree: selection.json owns the choice (model single|multiple, immediacy, variant, grouping) NOT applying an operation to the set; selection block (SelectionBehavior) owns multi-select mechanics + aria-multiselectable (the INPUT, not the action surface); command.json owns a single invocation (id/label/binding, Invoker Commands API, one CommandEvent) NOT fan across N; the action-bar container is already homed (assemblerPresets/toolbar.json + focus-delegation + composite-widget, the #1409 toolbar placement) so the bar is not the residual. Precise unowned residual = the batch-apply binding/semantics owned by neither selection nor command nor toolbar: (1) fan-out — apply one command across the live selection set's N targets, accepting the set as an id-set OR a select-all-matching predicate/exclusion-set; (2) select-all scope visible vs matching + the mixed header control + clear; (3) the 'N selected' aria-live=polite readout bound to selection-change; (4) post-action focus return after the set empties; (5) (optional) promoted-vs-overflow partitioning. Recommendation: (Fork 1, ~85%) a thin bulk-action INTENT composing selection + command over a target set, realized by a FUI behavior block — the selection extension/trait branch is BROKEN (forces selection, the choice, to own command fan-out, contradicting its scope and the command-orthogonality command.json asserts; selection must not depend on command); a headless behavior with no intent is BROKEN as the sole home (WE owns contracts; the recurring named pattern wouldn't be a WE standard — correct only as the impl half of the intent); the concern recurs independently (Polaris/Carbon name it) and owns net-new semantics (fan-out + select-all-matching predicate + count contract), clearing the bar for an intent not a mere recipe; residual (~15%) could be expressed as an assemblerPreset beside toolbar.json. (Fork 2, ~80%) expose a scope: visible | matching dimension, default visible (matching carries a predicate/exclusion-set not an id-list) — mandating one is broken (both ship deliberately, most-flexible-default); visible is the safe/accident-protected default, matching the author opt-in. Supported by default: 'N selected' via aria-live=polite; indeterminate header = aria-checked=mixed; multi-select input reuses SelectionBehavior; the bar reuses the existing toolbar (bulk-action BINDS the bar to the selection set, doesn't own the bar); each bar action is a command invocation that bulk-action fans; post-action focus return = a focus-delegation concern. Seams: selection (the choice; one-way dependency bulk-action -> selection; selection oblivious to commands) / command (single invoke; bulk-action's net-new verb is fan-out) / #1409 toolbar (the bar container; bulk-action owns the binding + batch semantics) / a future #1395-style optimistic mutation (a batch of N mutations; bulk-action NAMES the partial-failure outcome per-target and DELEGATES apply/rollback). Report: reports/2026-06-21-bulk-action-selection-fanout.md.
